subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: governance/rights/politics rebuilt from independent sources (IMF Art IV 2025, World Bank, OECD, Freedom House, RSF, CPJ, Transparency International, HRW, Nikkei Asia, RFA, The Diplomat); Vietnamese party/state media EXCLUDED there, with economic data sourced to IMF/World Bank.
A one-party communist state: scores reflect observable actions and independent data, not state messaging.

Standout drags: very low press freedom and civil liberties, a banking mega-fraud aftermath, severe Mekong/typhoon climate exposure, and a closing demographic window. Offsets: prudent public finances, a capable developmental state, and a strong export trajectory.

economic.notes: Prudent public finances and a strong export trajectory, against a banking mega-fraud aftermath and limited monetary independence.

fiscal_state.notes: Public and guaranteed debt fell to ~31% GDP with a deficit ~1.5-2.3% and a low risk of sovereign distress - genuinely prudent. (IMF 2025 Art IV)

monetary_independence.notes: A tightly managed band rather than a free float, with thin reserves (~2.3 months of imports) and sizeable FX intervention capping policy room. (IMF; OECD)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The dong is not a reserve currency. (IMF)

inflation.notes: CPI ~3.3-3.6%, below the target ceiling - controlled. (IMF; World Bank)

banking_stability.notes: A mega-fraud aftermath forced central-bank special loans worth ~5.6% of GDP, with a multi-year toxic-loan cleanup amid rapid credit growth and property/bond stress - a real systemic tail. (Nikkei; IMF)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: A fast-growing manufacturing/export powerhouse and a China+1 beneficiary, against heavy single-market US-tariff exposure (a 2025 reciprocal tariff plus transshipment scrutiny); no resource curse. (USTR)

institutional.notes: A capable developmental state and usable economic data, against no judicial independence and the absence of independent media.

rule_of_law.notes: A one-party state with no judicial independence; the anti-graft drive is also factional, though commercial-law reforms function (TI CPI ~41). (Transparency International 2025; HRW)

statistical_integrity.notes: Economic data is IMF/World-Bank-validated; governance data is opaque. (IMF Art IV)

civil_service_capacity.notes: A capable developmental state, executing a 2025 mega-restructuring of provinces and staffing - a genuine strength. (East Asia Forum; The Diplomat)

press_freedom.notes: RSF 174/180, with no independent media and jailed bloggers. (RSF 2026; CPJ)

political_social.notes: Orderly, low-crime managed stability, against severely restricted liberties and a closing demographic window.

political_stability.notes: High single-party control - managed stability - amid 2024-25 leadership consolidation ahead of the 2026 party congress; stable but not pluralist. (RFA; The Diplomat)

civil_liberties.notes: Rated 'Not Free,' with 2025 described as the most severe repression in decades. (Freedom House 2025)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Work permits and temporary residence are reasonably attainable for professionals, but there is no real path to citizenship or belonging in a one-party state. (observed)

demographics.notes: Still a young-ish dividend now, but ageing faster than it is getting rich - a closing window. (UN; World Bank)

geopolitical.notes: Non-aligned 'bamboo diplomacy', against active South China Sea disputes and capital controls.

alliance_reliability.notes: 'Bamboo diplomacy' - non-aligned, hedging the US and China via comprehensive partnerships - independent but not a treaty anchor. (observed)

conflict_involvement.notes: Active South China Sea disputes with China, including competing reclamation and coast-guard harassment - a real tail. (CNN; RFA)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: A managed capital account and FX controls - orderly but not open. (IMF)

physical_practical.notes: Low crime and fast-improving infrastructure, against severe Mekong/typhoon climate exposure and thin catastrophe insurance.

climate.notes: Among the world's most climate-exposed: Mekong Delta subsidence outpacing sea-level rise, salinity threatening the rice bowl, and intensifying typhoons. (World Bank; Nature)

healthcare.notes: Near-universal coverage, improving but uneven and low-spend. (World Bank)

infrastructure.notes: Improving fast, with power-supply reliability a concern. (OECD)

crime_safety.notes: Low street crime - a clear strength. (observed)

disaster_insurance.notes: Low penetration against very high exposure. (observed)

summary: A fast-growing manufacturing export powerhouse and China+1 beneficiary with genuinely prudent public finances (low debt, controlled inflation), a capable developmental state, and very low street crime. The drags are political and structural: a one-party state with no judicial independence, very low press freedom and 'Not Free' civil liberties, a banking mega-fraud aftermath requiring large central-bank support, heavy single-market US-tariff exposure, severe Mekong-Delta and typhoon climate exposure, and a window that closes as it ages before getting rich. Professionals can work but cannot belong.
